title: Thoughts on Developer Tooling
excerpt: Good developer tooling is invisible when it works and painful when it doesn't. Here are some patterns I've found consistently valuable.
ai_summary: This post argues that the best developer tools reduce friction without demanding attention. It covers CLI ergonomics, fast feedback loops, and the value of opinionated defaults in backend workflows.

title: Patterns for Resilient Backend Systems
excerpt: A look at circuit breakers, retry strategies, and idempotency keys as core tools for building fault-tolerant services.
ai_summary: This post walks through three foundational patterns for resilient distributed services: circuit breakers to stop cascading failures, exponential backoff for safe retries, and idempotency keys to make operations safe to repeat.
